name: Alagoas (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the blood of those who fought for the homeland

description: Three equal vertical stripes of red, white and blue, with the coat of arms of the State of Alagoas centred on the white stripe.
history: Adopted as the flag of the State of Alagoas on 30 September 1894 by State Law No. 88; the design preserves the colours used by Alagoas after its separation from Pernambuco in 1817.

features.symbolism: Three vertical stripes of red, white and blue, the colours of liberty
Centred on the white stripe, the state arms display sugar-cane and cotton branches surrounding a shield with sailing vessels and the date 16 September 1817
